reasons: Baxia reduces the value of Rafaela's healing during skirmishes.
Rafaela's team wants to reset through sustain and movement speed, while Baxia can force lower-value healing windows.

proof.summary: Rafaela wants to restore allies during skirmishes, while Baxia reduces healing and regeneration so her team loses sustain advantage.
worksBestWhen: Baxia tags Rafaela's grouped allies before the main damage exchange.
Allies commit damage during the anti-heal window.
failureCases: Rafaela's team disengages before Baxia reaches them.
Baxia cannot apply pressure to the correct targets.

reasons: Rafaela teams often group to receive healing and speed value.
Luo Yi can punish grouped movement with mark reactions and displacement.

proof.summary: Rafaela wants allies close enough to receive support value, while Luo Yi punishes clustered positioning with Yin-Yang reactions.
worksBestWhen: Rafaela's team groups around objectives or jungle entrances.
Luo Yi has follow-up damage ready after the pull or stun.
failureCases: Rafaela's team spreads before Luo Yi marks multiple targets.
Luo Yi misses mark timing or lacks follow-up.

reasons: Natalia can pressure Rafaela's backline position and force her to play defensively.
Rafaela loses a lot of team value if she is zoned away before a fight starts.

proof.summary: Rafaela wants safe backline access to allies, while Natalia creates hidden pickoff pressure that can remove or zone her before a fight.
worksBestWhen: Natalia controls flank bushes before objectives.
Rafaela is separated from tank or damage core.
failureCases: Rafaela stays grouped with hard crowd-control peel.

reasons: Saber can remove Rafaela quickly before her team benefits from repeated healing and speed boosts.
This works best when Rafaela is exposed or lacks defensive peel.

proof.summary: Rafaela wants to stay alive behind her team and provide repeated support value, while Saber can target and burst her when she is exposed.
worksBestWhen: Saber attacks from fog after Rafaela steps forward.
Rafaela has no nearby peel or defensive cooldown.
failureCases: Rafaela stays behind a tank and Saber cannot reach her.
Saber dives too deep and gets traded after the burst.

reasons: Franco can punish Rafaela's positioning with hook or suppression before she can reset her team.
Pickoff pressure forces Rafaela to play safer and reduces her ability to control vision with her team.

proof.summary: Rafaela wants to rotate with allies and support from safe range, while Franco can punish exposed support movement with hook or suppression.
worksBestWhen: Franco controls bush vision before Rafaela rotates.
Allies follow up immediately after the pickoff.
failureCases: Franco hooks a tank instead of Rafaela.
Rafaela stays behind teammates and avoids hook angles.
